programme.title: AWA Classic Wrestling - The Best of Jesse The Body Ventura
AWA Road Warriors Road to the Championship - https://archive.org/details/youtube-476hAbFLs5w
NWA AWA Nite of Champions II
Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: There is a new criminal mastermind at large (Professor Moriarty) and not only is he Holmes’ intellectual equal, but his capacity for evil and lack of conscience may give him an advantage over the detective.

programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: When a virus leaks from a top-secret facility, turning all resident researchers into ravenous zombies and their lab animals into mutated hounds from hell, the government sends in an elite military task force to contain the outbreak.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: Every Which Way But Loose
programme.desc: Philo Beddoe is your regular, easygoing, truck-driving guy. He's also the best bar-room brawler west of the Rockies. And he lives with a 165-pound orangutan named Clyde. Like other guys, Philo finally falls in love - with a flighty singer who leads him on a screwball chase across the American Southwest. Nothing's in the way except a motorcycle gang, some cops, and legendary brawler Tank Murdock.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: In a future where a failed global-warming experiment kills off most life on the planet, a class system evolves aboard the Snowpiercer; a train that travels around the globe via a perpetual-motion engine.

programme.title: Predator
programme.desc: A team of commandos on a mission in a Central American jungle find themselves hunted by an extraterrestrial warrior.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
programme.desc: In this third installment of the adrenaline-fueled action franchise, skilled assassin John Wick (Keanu Reeves) returns with a $14 million price tag on his head and an army of bounty-hunting killers on his trail. After killing a member of the shadowy international assassin's guild, the High Table, John Wick is excommunicado, but the world's most ruthless hit men and women await his every turn.

programme.title: Pitch Black
programme.desc: A transport ship crashes and leaves its crew stranded on a desert planet inhabited by bloodthirsty creatures that come out during an eclipse.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: After being bitten by a genetically-modified spider, a shy teenager gains spider-like abilities that he uses to fight injustice as a masked superhero and face a vengeful enemy.

programme.title: Back to School
programme.desc: Millionaire businessman Thornton Melon is upset when his son Jason announces that he is not sure about going to college. Thornton insists that college is the best thing he never had for himself, and to prove his point, he agrees to enroll in school along with his son. Thornton is a big hit on campus: always throwing the biggest parties, knowing all the right people, but is this the way to pass college?

programme.title: Austin Powers in Goldmember
programme.desc: The world's most shagadelic spy continues his fight against Dr. Evil. This time, the diabolical doctor and his clone, Mini-Me, team up with a new foe—'70s kingpin Goldmember. While pursuing the team of villains to stop them from world domination, Austin gets help from his dad and an old girlfriend.

programme.title: Happy Gilmore
programme.desc: A Hockey player wannabe finds out that he has the most powerful golf drive in history. He joins the P.G.A. tour to make some money to save grandma's house. The downside is that his hockey player mentality doesn't really go on the P.G.A. tour. Especially with the favorite to win the championship.i

programme.title: The Toy
programme.desc: On one of his bratty son Eric's annual visits, the plutocrat U.S. Bates takes him to his department store and offers him anything in it as a gift. Eric chooses a black janitor who has made him laugh with his antics. At first the man suffers many indignities as Eric's "toy," but gradually teaches the lonely boy what it is like to have and to be a friend.

programme.title: Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me
programme.desc: When diabolical genius Dr. Evil travels back in time to steal superspy Austin Powers's ‘mojo,’ Austin must return to the swingin' '60s himself - with the help of American agent, Felicity Shagwell - to stop the dastardly plan. Once there, Austin faces off against Dr. Evil's army of minions to try to save the world in his own unbelievably groovy way.

programme.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail 1974
programme.desc: King Arthur, accompanied by his squire, recruits his Knights of the Round Table, including Sir Bedevere the Wise, Sir Lancelot the Brave, Sir Robin the Not-Quite-So-Brave-As-Sir-Lancelot and Sir Galahad the Pure. On the way, Arthur battles the Black Knight who, despite having had all his limbs chopped off, insists he can still fight. They reach Camelot, but Arthur decides not to enter, as "it is a silly place".

programme.title: Idiocracy
programme.desc: Corporal Joe Bauers, a decidedly average American, is selected for a top-secret hibernation program but is forgotten and left to awaken to a future so incredibly moronic that he's easily the most intelligent person alive.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: Home Alone
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Kevin McCallister makes the most of the situation after his family unwittingly leaves him behind when they go on Christmas vacation. When thieves try to break into his home, he puts up a fight like no other.

programme.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off
programme.desc: A brash, cocky high school senior, tired of skipping school to spend a boring day at home, is determined to enjoy an epic day roaring around his favorite Chicago sites, enlisting his best friend and girlfriend to join him on the adventure.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Monty Python's Life of Brian
programme.desc: Brian Cohen is an average young Jewish man, but through a series of ridiculous events, he gains a reputation as the Messiah. When he's not dodging his followers or being scolded by his shrill mother, the hapless Brian has to contend with the pompous Pontius Pilate and acronym-obsessed members of a separatist movement. Rife with Monty Python's signature absurdity, the tale finds Brian's life paralleling Biblical lore, albeit with many more laughs.

programme.title: Spaceballs
programme.desc: When the nefarious Dark Helmet hatches a plan to snatch Princess Vespa and steal her planet's air, space-bum-for-hire Lone Starr and his clueless sidekick fly to the rescue. Along the way, they meet Yogurt, who puts Lone Starr wise to the power of "The Schwartz." Can he master it in time to save the day?

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop
programme.desc: Fast-talking, quick-thinking Detroit street cop Axel Foley has bent more than a few rules and regs in his time, but when his best friend is murdered, he heads to sunny Beverly Hills to work the case like only he can.

programme.title: Friday After Next
programme.desc: Craig and Day Day have finally moved out of their parents houses and into their own crib. The cousins work nights at a local mall as security guards. When their house is robbed on Christmas Eve they team up to track him down.

programme.title: The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad!
programme.desc: A rerun of many of the gags from the television series Police Squad! (1982). An Airplane! (1980)-type spoof, this time with the an incompetent Lieutenant Frank Drebin, who always "gets his man". Visual gags come thick and fast, and it's impossible to catch them all with one viewing. The plot: Queen Elizabeth II of England is coming to town, and Vincent Ludwig has plans to assassinate her using a brainwashed baseball player.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: Seeking shelter from a storm, five travelers are in for a bizarre and terrifying night when they stumble upon the Femm family estate.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
programme.desc: Slim and Tubby are American cops in London to study police tactics. They wind up in jail and are bailed out by Dr. Jekyll. Jekyll has been murdering fellow doctors who laugh at his experiments. He has more murders in mind. At one point the serum that turns Jekyll into the murderous Hyde gets injected into Tubby.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital, clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Five grisly tales from a kid's comic book about a murdered father rising from his grave, a bizarre meteor, a vengeful husband, a mysterious crate's occupant, and a plague of cockroaches.

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Transylvanian vampire Count Dracula bends a naive real estate agent to his will, then takes up residence at a London estate where he sleeps in his coffin by day and searches for potential victims by night.

programme.title: Ed Wood
programme.desc: Ambitious but troubled movie director Edward D. Wood Jr. tries his best to fulfill his dreams despite his lack of talent.

programme.title: Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
G Love and Special Sauce - Woodstock 99 Act 03
Don Kirshner's Rock Concert - Steppenwolf-Ufo-Bad Company-Foghat Live (1974)
The Golden Girls
programme.desc: The girls each plan to spend Christmas with their families but a series of strange occurrences lead them to spend Christmas together in Miami.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Rose adopts a delusional elderly woman who thinks she's her daughter; Sophia and Chuy decide to go into the Chinese takeout business.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont pays $50 for an antique gun and brings it home to show Fred. Fred begins re-enacting war stories and accidentally pulls the trigger not knowing it's loaded. The blast shoots through the next door neighbor's window, who suddenly drops from site just after having a heated argument with Fred. Fred jumps to the conclusion that he killed him.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: The family is getting ready to celebrate Mike's good fortune now that he's been published. But the timing is not good because Archie has just been laid off due to the cancellation of a government contract.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie continues to have problems with finances and with finding another job. At the grocery store, he stresses over Edith's spending habits and tries to save money by denting cans to save two cents each.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Still unemployed, Archie checks into the hospital for gall bladder surgery, a situation that proves to be anything but routine. Not only does he find Edith reading a Playgirl in the waiting room, he has to deal with a smart-alack Puerto Rican nurse and then a burly West Indian surgical assistant who preps him for surgery by giving him a shave . . . but not on his chin.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Denise & Paul vs. Cheryl & Heidi vs. Lisa & Sue

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Franny & Jack vs. Missy Scott vs. Jennifer & Jonathan

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Larry is a womanizer who is having an affair with Moe's wife. At the same time, he is also making eyes at Joe's fiancée, Millie.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are artists living in Paris. When the landlord comes after the overdue rent, the boys skip out and wind up joining the French Foreign Legion. Posted to the desert, their assignment is to guard captain Gorgonzola from the natives. When the captain is kidnapped, the boys must disguise themselves as harem girls to infiltrate the chieftains hideout and rescue him.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are the Wrong brothers, three inventors trying to finish building an airplane they can sell to the army and thereby avoid the draft. When their plane, the "Buzzard", turns out to be a flop, the boys are drafted into the army where they have trouble with a tough drill sergeant.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: A rich woman hires the Stooges (who are pest exterminators) to escort her to a fancy party.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are phone repairmen who are mistaken for the psychiatrists in whose office they are working. A rich man hires them to treat his impetuous young wife who is always running of for submarine rides and the like. The boys ruin a dinner party at their clients mansion but their antics so amuse his wife the she is cured and the stooges are paid off handsomely.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Lawbreakers in England, the Stooges are sent by the government to the new colonies to help fight the ""red man savage.""

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: General Muster dispatches the Stooges to catch cattle rustlers. Curly is Buffalo Billius, Moe is Wild Bill Hiccup and Larry is Just Plain Bill.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne tries her hand at telephone solicitation while trying to console Becky who had an embarrassing accident in front of the school council.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Jackie takes umbrage at Roseanne's remarks about her career ambitions when she reveals that she has enrolled in police academy; Becky gets caught reading Dan's girlie magazine.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Inexperienced Roseanne is hired for -- and subsequently fired from -- a job requiring computer experience.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: After a lonely architect who works with Dan has dinner at the Conners', he attempts to buy their friendship.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne begins to feel the empty-nest syndrome when Jackie departs for police training, Becky dates a 17-year-old and Darlene goes to a co-ed party.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: A kiss is just a kiss, but it could mean trouble when the kisser is Dan's buddy Arnie, and the kissee is Roseanne.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The Conner family terrify each other out of their wits on Halloween night.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne tries to get a few minutes of relaxation in the bathtub, but she ends up getting constant interruptions from her family--and a fantasy about two hunks.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The standard of the canteen food of Grace Brothers is rapidly going from bad to worse. After confronting the catering workers with their complaints, the staff are told to get on with the food preparation on their own.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Because of the poor wages at Grace Brothers, Mr.Goldberg has started an employment agency and is helping practically the whole of Grace Brothers find better paying jobs. His special new clients are Mr.Humphries and Mrs. Slocomb. But when management gets wind of the latest news, the employees might just be forced to find new jobs.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Toony learns something new about Bill when they play "Two Truths and a Lie."
Featured cartoons: Rabbit Fire (1951) Banty Raids (1963) The Paneless Window Washer (1937) Easy Peckin's (1953) A Pair of Sneakers (1969) The Missing Mouse (1953)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: The mistress of a conductor becomes a target for murder after threatening to tell all to her lover's wife.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude plans a surprise 50th birthday bash for Walter that does not go as well as she planned since Walter is too depressed to have any fun. However, Maude hopes an old friend will be the cure for Walter's gloominess, but she is dead wrong when he drops dead before Walter's eyes.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Walter and Maude are planning on taking a second honeymoon to Italy, however, the day their plane leaves, Maude gets a tetnus shot which results in a rash. She immediatley hotfoots it down to the doctors office where she encounters a busy preoccupied doctor who perscribes her medication but doesn't advise her on how to take it, resulting in an overdose, cancellation of the honeymoon and a possible malpractice suit.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: For Sophia's birthday, Dorothy flies in her sister, Angela from Sicily as a surprise. However, Dorothy's the one whose surprised when Sophia's reaction is that of disgust.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.sub-title: The Stan Who Came To Dinner
programme.desc: Dorothy feels sympathy for Stan when he tells her he's going to have open heart surgery. However that sympathy fades when she learns of his many infidelities while they were married.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host another exciting edition of Prime Time Wrestling, featuring an incredible lineup of in-ring confrontations. The Barbarian goes one-on-one with Hercules. King Haku takes on Big John Studd, and much more. Plus, the anticipation for the upcoming release of No Holds Barred continues to build.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: The new health inspector proves to be a decent fellow, until he blackmails Bob into being allowed to perform his atrocious music at the restaurant, which drives away the customers.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray witnesses Debra's hypersensitive mood and generally annoyed reaction to everything he says and does, he checks the calendar and realizes it's going to be a long couple of days ahead. In his desperate attempt for a "quick fix," he makes a bad situation worse by suggesting that a simple pill will help with his wife's insufferable mood swings.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: During his first day back on active duty, Robert loses his nerve and decides to quit the police force.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Deacon, who are out of work because of a strike, resort to playing sophomoric pranks, along with Arthur, to keep from going stir crazy. But they're driving Carrie and Kelly nuts. Eventually, Doug and Deacon return to work leaving Arthur alone.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After a great Thanksgiving, Doug and Carrie try to recall their first Thanksgiving together and who said, "I Love You" first. They consult Arthur, who quickly recalls some other Thanksgivings and finally recalls 1993 when he was having a fight with his new wife, Lily (Florence Henderson), as Doug was coming over to meet the family.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer sues Elaine's friend for sinking his golf career; George's apparent work ethic impresses his bosses.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer demands compensation from Elaine for his chiropractic effort; Jerry's flame always wears the same outfit.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Fred asks Roseanne how to propose to Jackie, then uses hints from Jackie's favourite soap opera; an enraged Roseanne demands romance from Dan, then turns to fantasy; Mark and David struggle with the concepts of feminism.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Egos clash when Jackie and Fred can't make a decision about their marriage. Fred is having what Dan calls the moment of clarity, a case of the jitters that affects some men just before the big day. But what's really bothering Fred is his belief that Jackie has feelings for someone she sees almost every day and whom she is always asking to come over and fix stuff, none other than Dan.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael expects praise but receives month-old evaluation notes; his whirlwind performance review includes Dwight's compliments, Jan's disapproval, and a parking-lot rant that leaves Pam and Jim hopelessly amused.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael starts monitoring the emails sent out in the Dunder-Mifflin workplace, upsetting the employees. Meanwhile, Pam believes that the relationship between Dwight and Angela is more than just worker to worker, Jim throws an office BBQ at his house in order to show his roommate the kinds of people he deals with everyday, and Michael, after annoying everyone at his improv classes, crashes Jim's party with a terrible karaoke number.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer's rush to the hospital to reattach his thumb, Lisa's rush to school to win the science fair, and Bart's run-in with an illegal fireworks scheme are interconnected in a parody of Go and Run Lola Run.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After finding a sketchbook belonging to his late wife Maude, Ned Flanders opens a Bible-themed amusement park to honor her memory.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After spraining his knee during a basketball game, Homer begins taking care of the neighborhood kids to cure his boredom, prompting jealousy from Bart and Lisa, who feel that Homer is giving the kids the attention they never had.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer refuses to pay a five dollar airport tax to fly to Delaware, the family is forced to ride in a livestock car of a train instead. There they meet a singing hobo who tells three tall tales: Homer as Paul Bunyan. Lisa as Connie Appleseed, a parody of Johnny Appleseed. Bart as Tom Sawyer with Nelson as Huckleberry Finn.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc:
In the twelfth Treehouse of Horror episode:
Hex and the City – While on a day trip through Ethnictown, Homer's bumbling catches the ire of a gypsy, who curses Homer's family and friends into receiving nothing but bad luck.
House of Whacks – in this mixed parody of Demon Seed and 2001: A Space Odyssey, Marge buys an automated house and customizes it with the Pierce Brosnan personality, who falls for Marge and attempts to murder Homer.
Wiz Kids – In this Harry Potter parody, Bart and Lisa go to a school for wizards, and Lord Montemort (Mr. Burns) uses Bart to capture Lisa's magic.
Guest star: Pierce Brosnan and Matthew Perry.[20]

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Kelly breaks a date to accept her parents invitation to celebrate her 21st birthday by going to the movies with them, where she finds her boyfriend there with another woman. Meanwhile, Bud tries unsuccessfully (several times) to find a girl to sit next to him, and Al is bored that the movie has no sex or violence in it.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al's poor bedroom performance makes him the laughingstock of the entire town after another night with Peggy, even though the only person she tells about it is Marcy. Al then decides to get in shape for the real deal to get his self-confidence back.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al takes a $12,000 offer for early retirement from the shoe store, but Peggy finds the money and spends all of it in one hour by shopping. Al is forced to go back to work where he hears opportunity knocking with a chance to buy a shoe store called "Home Plate Athletic Shoes." Meanwhile, Charlene Tilton visits the Bundy house trying to sell them an "Abdomenizer."

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: One morning, Al discovers that his Dodge has been stolen. At Jefferson's behest, Al decides to cheat the insurance company and enjoys renting a loaner Lincoln Town Car...Until the police find his beloved heap.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Bud's cousin gets divorced after finding out that his wife did the deed with Bud just before their wedding. Or rather, he doesn't exactly know it was Bud, but he's determined to find out. As Bud waits for the hammer fist to fall he turns to Al, of all people, to discuss the morality of his actions.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al's old girlfriend wants to buy Al from Peggy for $500,000.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: A Tisket, a Tasket, Can Peg Make a Basket?
programme.desc: When Al manages to get two prime tickets to a charity all-star basketball game, Peggy spoils his fun by insisting she come along. Things turn even worse for Al when she trades seats with him just before his former seat is chosen for a $10,000 free-throw contest. Meanwhile, Kelly tries to get herself basketball player Vlade Divac.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al is thrilled when his childhood friend asks for his help in dealing with a young thug and his gang who have been harassing her at the inner city convenience store where she now works. But how does Al explain it to Peggy? Simply put: Al checks himself and Peggy into a local hotel near the rough neighborhood and sneaks out to confront the thug Ray-Ray and his gang, only to get beaten up by the youths again and again.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred gets a letter in the mail from someone named Carol who claims that Fred knew her 40 years ago and that she's coming for a visit. However, Fred doesn't recall knowing any Carols and is in for a shock when Carol turns out to be a man. Another shock, Carol claims that he and Fred robbed a bank years ago.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Aunt Esther and Woody have decided to have a baby and set up an appointment with an adoption agency to adopt an older child. However, on the day of a preliminary interview, Woody, so nervous about the prospects of being a father, gets drunk, threatening their chances for an adoption. A heartbroken Esther turns to Fred for help.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: The day has come for Aunt Esther and Woody to meet their son but they are in for a shock when he helps himself to their cash register. Later, Esther is in for an even bigger shock when he announces that he doesn't believe in God. Esther promptly kicks him out and he takes refuge at the Sanford residence.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont runs into Chuck Barris in a parking lot and he generously gives Lamont four tickets to a taping of The Gong Show, which happens to be Fred's favorite game show. Fred decides to try to be a contestant and sets up an act with his brother-in-law, Rodney and Lamont. However, at the last minute, Rodney hurts his back and can't be in the act.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Sharon's sister, Charleen, comes to visit and she is taken with Chris' charm, much to Sharon's chagrin.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris tries to raise money to help save a playground, so he tries to set a world record by having the most things piled on top of him.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs helps a losing team get some runs by helping everyone all around the field.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: To the tune of The Nutcracker, a number of elves do all the work in a shoe shop.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: The overworked baby delivery system has some issues to fix.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Daffy Duck is on a rampage, painting mustaches on every face, with Policeman Porky Pig in pursuit.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A group of celebrity dogs decide that they need a nightclub of their own.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs is lured into an evil scientist's lair, and starts to be chased by a big and hairy orange monster. The scientist wants the monster to catch Bugs so that he can use the bunny for his next experiment.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky puts his cats out in the snow, but then they put him out and have a party. Expelling them again, Porky goes to bed, only to be terrorized by the felines' mock Martian invasion.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Seeking fame and fortune in the picture business, the little black duck arrives in Hollywood and makes a bee line for Warmer Brothers studio where he hopes to meet the stars and land a contract. However, the studio has a guard who is determined not to let anyone in

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Hilarity ensues as some major intestinal cramps convince Beavis that he might be pregnant.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys are supposed to sell candy for school, but end up only selling it to each other -- with the same two dollars.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Buzzcut demands a report about our country's forefathers from the boys; they imagine themselves as various figures in American history.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis & Butt-head visit a phony fortune teller who cons Beavis into thinking he has the gift of second sight.
videos:
Metallica - "For Whom the Bell Tolls"
Nina Hagen - "Herman Was His Name"
Donald Fagen - "Snowbound"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Van Driessen holds a mock graduation to build the class's self-esteem. Beavis and Butt-head think it's the real thing and rejoice that they can get on with their lives.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The school psychiatrist requires Beavis, Butt-head and Stewart to go on a canoe trip, led by Mr. Van Driessen. Butt-head tells Beavis that this is Mr. Van Driessen's attempt at making ""his move"" on Beavis. A fight ensues, and the boys' canoe capsizes. While relieving themselves in the woods (wearing only underwear), they get poison ivy. Mr Van Driessen gets mauled by a bear.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The two boys cause huge chaos around Highland playing with a big tire they find at a garage.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis and Butt-head go to Driver's Ed. class and watch a gory 1950's State Highway Patrol movie about reckless drivers...featuring some very familiar greasers.
videos:
Shonen Knife - "Tomato Head"
Alice Cooper - "Lost In America"
Johnny Cash - "Delia's Gone"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head fill their house with garbage to meet girls.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis deep fries a mouse and bugs, and Butt-head serves it to Mr. Anderson as a mouse-burger and French flies.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head finally manage to get their hands on some (non-alcoholic) beer and get "fit-shaced".

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head pledge money to Daria for a walkathon, then have to walk off the money when they can't pay.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: When Pee-wee wants a letter mailed, he has Jambi make Reba appear right inside the Playhouse! Because it's her day off, Pee-wee makes her breakfast... with imaginary food! Later, Pterri feels unwanted and runs away from home. Secret Word: NOW.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee has to eat his words when he tells the gang how important dental hygiene is and, then, develops an ow-some toothache! Since Jambi is taking a head-turning vacation at Mount Rushmore, Pee-Wee is forced to face his greatest fear - the dentist! He's so afraid, it'll take Miss Yvonne's coaxing just to get him to go. When he finally does make "it," Pee-wee learns that visiting the dentist is nothing to grit his teeth over and can actually be fun.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: As Caroline and Max deep-clean part of the diner for Han before a health inspection, they make a discovery that could help relaunch their cupcake business; Sophie and Oleg break up after he cheats on her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Word about a shocking incident involving a celebrity brings customers to Max and Caroline's new cupcake window.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline tries to raise money for an expensive pair of new pants through an online fundraising site. Meanwhile, Max’s new smartphone comes between her and Caroline.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host another extraordinary lineup of wrestling action, including King Haku going one-on-one with "Hacksaw" Jim Duggan. Plus, The Blue Blazer & Koko B. Ware take on The Brain Busters, an update on the rivalry between Jake "The Snake" Roberts and "The Million Dollar Man" Ted DiBiase, and more.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As Rhaenyra looks to gain an advantage by unusual means, Daemon pressures a young liege lord to raise up his bannermen.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: The Baratheon rivalry ends; Tyrion learns of Cersei's secret weapon; Dany suffers a loss; Arya collects a debt she didn't know was owed.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: The Old Gods and the New
programme.desc: Arya has a surprise visitor; Dany vows to take what is hers; Joffrey meets his subjects; Qhorin gives Jon a chance to prove himself.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Wilson prepares to transplant bone marrow from Matty to his brother Nick. But when Matty gets sick, the team must cure him as he's the only safe donor. Meanwhile Foreman deals with the consequences of his mistake and House has a battle of wills with his new pet Hector.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The team deals with a 19-year-old college student, while Foreman suddenly and without a reason hands in his resignation. House seems to have already found Foreman's replacement, an attractive nutritionist named Honey.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and his friends go to the Super Bowl but when their tickets turn out to be fake they get thrown into jail. Fortunately they are freed by Dolly Parton and make their way to a director's box but this is owned by the 'billionaire tyrant' Rupert Murdoch, who orders the visitors' arrested. After being chased onto the field ,Homer and his friends cause considerable confusion which ends with Homer in possession of the Vince Lombardi trophy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A new TV series features a suave and debonair character named Homer Simpson. Homer capitalizes on his new namesake, until the character is rewritten to be a bumbling fool. Trying to recapture the positive attention he enjoyed, Homer changes his name to "Max Power."

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Apu is making the husbands of Springfield look bad with his extravagent Valentine's Day efforts for Manjula. When the men try to sabotage Apu's grand gesture, they accidentally wind up benefiting from it.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer buys a Canyonero utility vehicle but finds it was intended for women to drive so he gives it to Marge. Marge soon gets into her new vehicle,becoming prone to road rage,for which she arrested by Wiggum. However,when the rest of the family are trapped by an escaped rhinoceros at the zoo,Marge's reckless driving comes in handy at saving them.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Indebted from a mishap at the Smithsonian, Homer lets a cell phone company put a transmitter in the house. Forced to share a room with Bart, Lisa resents Homer until a New Age experience gives her empathy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After beating Homer in a steak-eating competition, a trucker dies; Homer and Bart take over his rig to complete his shipment.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Reverend Lovejoy subjects the congregation to an extended Bible reading, the Simpsons fall asleep and have dreams based on Bible stories.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer's failed do-it-yourself project gets mistaken for "outsider art." Suddenly in the limelight, Homer must come up with new "works" to impress the art world.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Old Man and the 'C' Student
programme.desc: When Bart ruins Springfield's chance to host the Olympics, the kids are sentenced to community service work at the retirement home. To help the residents recapture their youth, Bart takes them on an ill-fated boat ride.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A new mogul wins the hearts of the townspeople with a shower of cash. Jealous, Mr. Burns asks Homer to help make him a beloved billionaire too, and they hatch a plan to bring the Loch Ness Monster to Springfield.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: The fire is burning inside of Vince McMahon with his sights set on Stone Cold Steve Austin, however, Austin plans to extinguish that fire with the use of a Zamboni. The Rock, Mankind & Ken Shamrock join forces to battle the Brothers of Destruction. Val Venis takes on X-Pac. Mark Henry clashes with Faarooq. Plus, The New Age Outlaws and much more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira’s campaign takes a turn when some of her lawn signs mysteriously disappear. David and Alexis must work together to get David’s boss out of legal trouble.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira go out for their anniversary and run into a couple they knew from their former life, while Alexis and David attend Mutt’s annual barn party, making up with some friends, and making out with others.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The attractive owner of a local candy store is sweet on Caroline, but she's too embarrassed to date him after she had a mishap in the store.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And the Three Boys With Wood
programme.desc: Caroline and Max ask two Amish boys to build a barn for Chestnut; Andy makes a discovery about Caroline.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline figure out a surprising fundraising solution when a potential storefront for Max's Homemade Cupcake becomes available for the girls to rent as the two go to great lengths to raise enough money to see their dream come true.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline decide to hire an intern to help take care of some of the menial tasks involved with the preparation for opening their new cupcake shop, but Max seems conflicted with her new managerial role as the boss

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline host a grand opening celebration at their new cupcake shop, and Max invites her former flames to show off her success.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max sees Sophie depressed after being swindled by the contractor in charge of building her dream house, she offers to make her a silent partner in the cupcake business, but Caroline and Sophie have very different visions for the company.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Desperate for a way to pay their overdue rent, Max and Caroline get involved with a group of sketchy customers willing to pay top dollar for their cupcakes. Recurring guest star RYAN HANSEN appears as Andy.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A relaxing getaway for two becomes an awkward vacation for three when Max, Caroline and Andy spend a weekend together in a cabin in the woods.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The diner gang helps Max and Caroline bake throughout the night in order to fulfill a gigantic cupcake order.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After learning that Candy Andy closed down his shop after their breakup, Caroline visits a psychic for a reading about her love life in the future.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max lands tickets to a music awards show in Los Angeles, she and Caroline meet rapper 2 Chainz during the cross-country flight on a private jet.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max pay a steep price for trying to remove a street performer from the entry of their shop.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are unable to pay the rent on their shop. A person offers to take their lease but Caroline thinks they should try to find the money. So she decides to ask her Aunt who's a successful businesswoman. But she refuses to help them. They try to ask her again but she's on medication and she reveals that she hates Caroline which is why she doesn't want to help her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline take office temp jobs. While Caroline tries to climb the corporate ladder, Max deals with a boss who makes inappropriate advances.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Han fires Caroline when she insults his diner, so Max tries to mend their relationship.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline is in a tizzy when she develops a rash after a romantic evening with her former boyfriend.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Gene Pontecorvo makes a final break from the mob life, while Junior's paranoid delusions could be the end of Tony.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: The family tries to cope with Tony's hospitalization and possibly impending death, while Tony begins to hallucinate a new life parallel to his own.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael's crisis forces Eleanor to assume the title of the neighborhood architect. Tahani makes a discovery about the new humans.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: With Eleanor assuming the role of the architect, the group adapts to the challenges facing them when four test subjects inhabit a new neighborhood under their supervision.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor, Michael, Janet and Tahani have their hands full when the new residents begin to show their true colors. Jason receives some unsettling news.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As Aemond becomes more volatile, Larys plots an escape, and Alicent grows more concerned about Helaena's safety. Flush with new power, Rhaenyra looks to press her advantage.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Gumball and Darwin begin to complain about every little thing in their lives, the people of Elmore City mistakenly assumes charity cases and put together a benefit to raise money for them, leaving them having to fix the mix-up.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin discover Principal Brown's diploma is fake, and Principal Brown is determined to keep their discovery a secret.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Mr. Robinson has a stress attack and needs a stuffing transfusion from his son Rocky the janitor. He won't take it unless Rocky gets a better job in life. Rocky gets one with Chanax, where he becomes a work drone. Gumball and Darwin now need to get Rocky's soul back from his boss in order to get him to surgery.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Doughnut Cop shows Gumball and Darwin that the smallest misdemeanor can lead to a breakdown in law.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: It's Mother's Day and kids put their moms through a series of tests to determine who has the World's Greatest Mom.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: How's This for a Special? Spaaaace (2)
programme.desc: After receiving a galactic crime alert, the Titans take off for an epic space adventure. The Titans have to stop Darkseid's latest scheme, but the excitement of the space adventure might be too much for Robin to handle.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When he isn't invited to Beast Boy's birthday party, Robin throws his own party with special guest Bob Uecker.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Slapping Butts and Celebrating for No Reason
programme.desc: When the Big Game is threatened, the Titans must infiltrate the football stadium and stop the attack.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Nostalgia Is Not a Substitute for an Actual Story
programme.desc: Encoded By RMTeam, http://RMZ.cr

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Encoded By RMTeam, http://RMZ.cr

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Encoded By RMTeam, http://RMZ.cr

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Encoded By RMTeam, http://RMZ.cr

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: I Used To Be a Peoples
programme.desc: Encoded By RMTeam, http://RMZ.cr

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin teaches the other Titans about the difference between the Imperial System and the Metric System.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Encoded By RMTeam, http://RMZ.cr

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren's jealousy drives him to distraction when day after day, the mailman dumps mountains of fan mail addressed to Stimpy only. Stimpy appeases Ren's feelings of worthlessness by appointing him president of the Official Stimpy Fan Club. Can Ren put his feelings aside in order to perform the delicate duties involved in receiving and replying to the inexhaustible stream of Stimpy's fan mail?

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy's U.S. visit to a young fan named Anthony threatens to destroy all his youthful fantasies, so Anthony's overprotective father threatens to destroy them!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: The pioneering spirit is abound in this re-telling of the colonization of the north American continent. With the motto "We Always Get Our Butts Kicked!", Ren and Stimpy set out across forests, deserts, mountains and vast snow fields atop lumbering yaks. Can they tap into the nation's vast natural resources to bring about prosperity?

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: After Stimpy gets out of the shower, he finds that there is not a pair of pants in the house. Ignoring Ren, who tells him that clothes are for humans, and that he doesn't need pants, he tries multiple times to find a pair of pants, without much luck. He is shunned by the outside world because he is nude. He eventually gets lost in the forest, cold, naked, and embarrassed.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren's feeling fine on his 10th birthday until Stimpy reminds him that he's 70 years old in "dog years". Ren prepares for death with a will, a fake funeral, and the search for the perfect coffin.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head get jobs working at a computer tech support center, a position for which they are woefully under-qualified.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys go on a field trip to a military base and wind up in the virtual pilot seats of drone planes on an Afghan mission, thinking that it is Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas with planes.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Sixty Minutes will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Lisa and Rodney keep trying to get Kevin to moon the cameras, but Christine stops them.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Incredible Hulk Runs out of Deodorant will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine brings in a box of cosmetics and explains the use of each one. She offers advise about the dangers of using corn chip flavored lipstick.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Pacman Eats General Hospital will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine plays ATARI for the entire show, thus demonstrating how video games are addictive. In the sketches, many references are made to the soap General Hospital, and how addictive that is.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Most Popular Kids Show on Television will not be seen at this time so that we may presentâ€¦
Ross holds a ""phone-in"" contest to see which cast member is the most popular. All the kids wear numbers so that viewers can clarify their voting. At the halfway point, Kevin (wearing 111) is first. In an attempt to get more votes, Christine trades her ballot number (872) for a lower number (0). In the end, Zilch (Darryl) wins the contest even though he wasn't in this episode. They discover he won because Christine wore his number zero.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Fred Sanford: World's Best Dressed Man will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast attempts to keep up with the latest fashions, which change about every two minutes.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Love Boat Runs Aground Fantasy Island will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine goes on a trip to the beach to relax, only to run into the entire cast and crew of YCDTOTV, who are filming an episode.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Price is Right Raises their Prices will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Things around the set begin to malfunction. The risers collapse and the set begins to collapse. In the end, the entire studio collapses.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Rogers Bullies the Neighborhood will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine takes a self-defense class in order to protect herself from bullying from the other cast members.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Big Bird Dances Swan Lake will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast examines plays, literature, music, art, and theater. Christine is introduced to ""water music"".

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Oral drillers take on spelunkers on this MXC affair when the dentists take on explorers. Also, Kenny demonstrates some of his new inventions... in which Vic needs some teamsters to help.
Games include: The Dash to Death; Hand Job; Wall Bangers; Sinkers & Floaters

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
MXC throws open their games to kids for our first "Children's Special" as the country children take on the city children and see where the best kids come from. Also, Vic and Kenny test out Vic's new invention... a career-choosing device for toddlers.
Games include: Escape From Foster Care; Lil' Sinkers and Floaters; Irritable Bowl Training; Rotating Surfboard of Death Jr.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Organized Crime vs. Weight Loss Industry
programme.desc: It's the classic battle of the feared mob vs. fat slobs as the Organized Crime takes on the Weight Loss Industry. Games - Dead Men Walking, Runaway Stump, Little Man in the Boat, and Tumbling Dominoes of Doom.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Novelty & Gift Industry vs. The Death Industry
programme.desc:
It's the people who make gag presents vs. the people that plant stiffs for profit in an aquatic-themed show. Meanwhile, Vic discovers the joys of Kenny Blankenship's dressing room.
Games include: Wet Spot; Circle Jerkers Ahoy; Elimination Idol; Wall Bangers

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: The Amusement Park Industry vs. The World's Oldest Profession
programme.desc:
The employees of amusement parks take on those who give sex for hire. Also, Vic and Kenny have a go at a new game featuring deadly lasers, tanks, and some clown on a giant ball.
Games include: Hosin' the Ho's; Eat Shitake; Brass Balls; Sinkers and Floaters

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Forensics experts take on kiddie entertainment folks as Vic and Kenny try to solve a murder on this special edition of "MXCSI".
Games include: Wall Bangers; Buck Off; Sinkers and Floaters; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Bush/Cheney supporters take on the Kerry/Edwards supporters in this special episode's battle between President Bush and Senator Kerry.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Pie in the Sky; Boulder Dash; Character Assassination

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
People who strike oil take on people who give face lifts in this MXC competition.
Games include: Roulective Surgery; Rotating Surfboards of Death; Dash to Death; Mudd Butlers

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The annual teachers-versus-deejays roller hockey game is coming, and there's a good chance the students will get to see Mr. DeMartino die of an aneurysm during the game—but Daria's going to miss it because she's been grounded by her parents.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jane's brother Trent, who's also the lead singer of local grunge band "Mystik Spiral" is offered the chance to play in the upcoming Alternapaloosa tour. Since Daria harbor's an undeniable crush on Trent, Jane offers to pay for the gas in their van and take her for their attempted tour. The fashion club, and other Lawndale High students try to go to Alternapaloosa as well.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jake's stress level is reaching critical mass, so the Morgendorffers go on a camping trip. Like that's gonna help.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale High is abuzz with homecoming preparations. The quarterback who led the team to a state championship three years ago is returning! Then he has a tragic accident, which ironically leads to sudden popularity for our Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria reluctantly gets involved in a poster contest in which she and Jane depict "Student Life At The Dawn of the Millennium"... rather negatively.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale High School goes on another field trip, this time to a local paint-ball course, with Helen & Jake as parental chaperones. The true savage nature of everybody's personality is revealed on this trip. Daria and Jane opt to sneak out toward another nearby tourist attraction that proves to be a complete rip-off.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn needs a quick passing grade in English, and ends up writing an impressive essay(by Lawndale standards, that is), but fears that it might give her a reputation as a nerd. Instead it gives her a new following as a trendy intellectual, something that Daria, a true intellectual can't stand. Since desperate times call for desperate measures, Daria is willing to consider such measures in order to persuade her sister to give up this facade.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and her family participate in a strange and frightening ritual known as a family wedding.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A massive allergy attack caused by an army of remote controlled pillows is causing havoc across London. Not even Danger Mouse and Penfold are immune to this latest scheme masterminded by Baron Silas Greenback.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Crumhorn invents a chemical that allows him to create land sharks to terrorize London. Things get even more complicated when Penfold gets into the formula.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A giant spaceship is spotted heading for Earth. It's part of the ""Intergalactic 147"" snooker game and it wants to put the Earth into a black hole.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Silas Greenback unleashes robots of destruction of increasing size against Danger Mouse, the greatest guardian of peace and justice in the civilized world. First, a duo of Sniver Snappers are delivered to DM's secret hideout. Next, Penfold is almost stomped by a pair of electronic boots. Finaly, Dangermouse comes face to faceplate with Big Tom, The Mouse Masher.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback plans to take over the world by combining the sound of 10.000 stolen bagpipes he has hold up in Castle McStrangle. Luckily, Dangermouse has a good set of lungs up his chest. That is, of course, as long as he and Penfold survive the attack of a mechanical Loch Ness Monster.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback captures Colonel K and has the Colonel send Danger Mouse and Penfold on holiday to a haunted castle in Transylvania.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Straw & Acid - Straw Oboe - Ant Farmers - Rolling Pin Code - Equal Solids - Hawaiian Volcano - Swallow Upside Down - Crush Egg

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Dry Ice Fog - Napkin Suction - Crawling Cell - Computer Graph - Starch Test - Lizard Autopsy - Find Your Pulse - What Makes Rain

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman tellls all about elephants, gets help from his mom in the Beakman family challenge and reveals the mystery of X-ray machines.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals the facts on skin, its functions and its reproduction, tells the truth about the "funny bone," and then explains why oxygen is so important.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals facts about bread, and Lester plays a housewife in a game show. Beakman then reveals the history and secrets behind measurements.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains the differences between magnets and electromagnets. Art Burn uses gelatin to create jello. Phoebe needs the senses of the body explored.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals the similarities between apes and humans with Eddie the chimpanzee as his guest. Meekman takes a painless eye-exam.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Don’t panic, but germs are all around you. Germs are bacteria, viruses, protozoa, and fungi, very small one-celled organisms. Your skin keeps most of these organisms from ever getting into your body. If germs slip inside, your body has some powerful weapons to attack and destroy them. And not all germs are bad; there are good germs, too, like the ones that live in our stomach and intestines – our guts.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: There are lots of different climates all over the world. Deserts are warm and dry. Temperate forests are cold and wet. Tropical rain forests are warm and wet. Animals and plants live in climates that are good places for them to live. Cacti wouldn’t grow too well in the Arctic, just like polar bears would over heat in the desert.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Nicole tells Gumball and Darwin to take out the trash by 5 o'clock, but Gumball and Darwin just can't overcome their procrastinator personalities. When she gets home with the groceries, they hide the trash bag, then Chase after the garbage truck. They manage to get it into the back, but when they're eating trash for supper, they realise it was the wrong bag.
